system.description: Named for the soldiers under the command of Achilles in the Trojan War, fighters of the Myrmidon style favor the spear and shield of a Greek hoplite. But aside from tight formations, Myrmidons also excel at single combat or skirmishing with these weapons. It seems to have influenced both the Roland and Old Styles.

benefit.description:
Myrmidons are trained in the use of a round shield. This means that they do not lose the usual 1 point of Advantage but do have a -1 Penalty to attempts to hide, acrobatics, (and other Career Tests the GM might find a large shield to hinder) and Initiative rolls. Swimming with the shield gives a -2 Penalty instead and no Penalty Die.

You have also learned to turn the extra weight of the shield from a liability into an asset. When using the shield to perform any Maneuver, your Might is considered 1 higher to resolve that action.
benefit.name: Shield Expertise

finalSecret.description: You have become an expert at using the shield and spear in harmony. Whenever you get a Mighty Success with the shield (whether this is for a Parry, Hilt Punch, Shove, or any other combat roll), you get a free Bladework or Hurl Spear attack with the spear.
finalSecret.name: My Spear and Shield are One
